title: Add hierarchical dashboard grouping by path, surface, scenario, and target
description: Upgrade Sentinel's dashboard so Atlas-era artifacts can be browsed as a hierarchy instead of only as flat screen slugs.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard grouping supports hierarchical surface paths and scenario variants
Target differences such as platform, device, or variant remain visible without collapsing the surface model back to flat slugs
Legacy flat catalogs still render intelligibly during migration
The dashboard output is good enough to support large review sessions without filename archaeology
proofRequirements: HTML fixture or snapshot demonstrates hierarchical rendering
docs explain how legacy and Atlas-backed outputs differ

notes.text: Execution started for hierarchical dashboard rendering. This slice upgrades catalog HTML output so Atlas-era artifacts can be browsed by path, surface, scenario, and target while legacy flat catalogs remain readable.

Completed the hierarchical dashboard slice by wiring Atlas fixtures through catalog:index, adding manifest/session referential validation, hardening artifact-path and HTML escaping, extending CLI/test coverage, and capturing fresh proof at ~/Desktop/screenshots/sentinel/atlas-dashboard.png.
